steps.title: Connect one solution to the discriminant
steps.content: A quadratic has exactly one real solution when its discriminant is $0$. For $-2x^2+20x+c=0$, use $a=-2$ and $b=20$.

steps.title: Confirm the choice
steps.content: Choice B is $-50$, so B is correct.

steps.title: Check the one-solution condition
steps.content: A zero discriminant means the parabola touches the $x$-axis once instead of crossing twice or missing it. With $c=-50$, the discriminant is $400+8(-50)=0$.
